part1.title: Friends and Family
questions: Do you prefer spending time with friends or family? Why?
How often do you keep in touch with your friends?
Have your friendships changed as you have grown older?
What qualities do you think are most important in a good friend?

part2.title: Describe a person who has had a positive influence on your life
part2.cue_card:
Describe a person who has had a positive influence on your life.

You should say:
- Who this person is
- How you know them
- What they did that influenced you
- And explain why their influence has been important to you

part3.title: Influence and Role Models
questions: Why do you think some people have more influence over others than other people do?
Do you think celebrities make good role models for young people? Why or why not?
How has the internet changed the way people are influenced by others?

part1.title: Studying and School
questions: What subject did you enjoy most at school and why?
Do you prefer studying alone or with others?
How do you usually prepare for an important exam?
Do you think the education system in your country is good?

part2.title: Describe a teacher who made a strong impression on you
part2.cue_card:
Describe a teacher who made a strong impression on you.

You should say:
- Who this teacher was
- What subject they taught
- What made them different from other teachers
- And explain why they made such a strong impression on you

part3.title: Teaching and Education
questions: What qualities do you think make an effective teacher?
Do you think technology will eventually replace teachers in the classroom?
How important is it for children to learn subjects like art and music alongside academic subjects?

part1.title: Technology in Daily Life
questions: How much time do you spend on your phone each day?
What kind of apps do you use most frequently?
Do you think people rely too much on technology these days?
Has technology made your daily life easier or more complicated?

part2.title: Describe a piece of technology that you find particularly useful
part2.cue_card:
Describe a piece of technology that you find particularly useful.

You should say:
- What it is
- How long you have been using it
- How you use it in your daily life
- And explain why you find it so useful

part3.title: Technology and Society
questions: How do you think artificial intelligence will change the way people work in the future?
Do you think social media has had a mostly positive or mostly negative effect on society?
Should governments regulate how technology companies collect and use personal data?

part1.title: The Environment
questions: How concerned are you personally about climate change?
Do you do anything in your daily life to help the environment?
Are there any natural places near where you live that you enjoy visiting?
Do you think people in your country care enough about environmental issues?

part2.title: Describe a time when you spent time in nature
part2.cue_card:
Describe a time when you spent time in nature.

You should say:
- Where you went
- Who you were with
- What you did there
- And explain how spending time in that natural environment made you feel

part3.title: Environment and Responsibility
questions: Who do you think bears the greatest responsibility for protecting the environment — individuals, businesses or governments?
Do you think economic development and environmental protection can coexist?
How effective do you think international agreements on climate change have been?

part1.title: Work and Jobs
questions: What do you do for work or what are you studying?
What aspects of your work or studies do you enjoy most?
What kind of job would you most like to have in the future?
Do you think it is better to work for yourself or for a company?

part2.title: Describe a job you would like to try in the future
part2.cue_card:
Describe a job you would like to try in the future.

You should say:
- What the job is
- What skills or qualifications it requires
- What you would do in this job on a typical day
- And explain why you would like to try this job

part3.title: Work and Society
questions: Do you think job satisfaction is more important than salary when choosing a career?
How has the concept of work changed over the past few decades?
Do you think the traditional nine to five working day will still exist in twenty years time?

part1.title: Health and Wellbeing
questions: Do you think you have a healthy lifestyle?
How do you usually relax after a stressful day?
Do you do any regular exercise? What kind?
How important is sleep to you and do you get enough of it?

part2.title: Describe something you do to stay healthy
part2.cue_card:
Describe something you do to stay healthy.

You should say:
- What it is
- How long you have been doing it
- How often you do it
- And explain why you think it is important for your health

part3.title: Health and Society
questions: Do you think governments should do more to encourage citizens to live healthier lives?
Why do you think rates of obesity and lifestyle-related diseases are increasing in many countries?
How has our understanding of what constitutes a healthy diet changed in recent years?

part1.title: Travel
questions: Do you enjoy travelling? Where have you been recently?
Do you prefer travelling alone or with other people?
What do you think is the best way to experience a new culture?
Is there a country you would really like to visit? Why?

part2.title: Describe a memorable journey or trip you have taken
part2.cue_card:
Describe a memorable journey or trip you have taken.

You should say:
- Where you went
- Who you travelled with
- What you did or saw there
- And explain why this trip was particularly memorable for you

part3.title: Travel and Globalisation
questions: Do you think international tourism has more positive or negative effects on local communities?
How has cheap air travel changed the way people think about visiting other countries?
Do you think cultural differences between countries are becoming smaller because of globalisation?

part1.title: Where You Live
questions: Do you live in a city or a smaller town or village?
What do you like most about where you currently live?
Has the area where you live changed much in recent years?
Would you prefer to live in the city centre or in the suburbs? Why?

part2.title: Describe a city you have visited that you particularly enjoyed
part2.cue_card:
Describe a city you have visited that you particularly enjoyed.

You should say:
- Which city it was
- When you went there
- What you did and saw there
- And explain what it was about this city that you particularly enjoyed

part3.title: Urban Development and Planning
questions: What do you think are the biggest challenges facing cities today?
How can city planners make urban areas more liveable for their residents?
Do you think the trend of people moving from rural areas to cities will continue in the future?

part1.title: Art and Culture
questions: Are you interested in any form of art such as painting, music or literature?
Did you do anything creative when you were a child?
Do you ever visit museums, galleries or cultural events?
Do you think it is important for people to have creative hobbies?

part2.title: Describe a work of art, music or literature that has had an impact on you
part2.cue_card:
Describe a work of art, music or literature that has had an impact on you.

You should say:
- What it is and who created it
- When you first encountered it
- What it is about or what it depicts
- And explain why it has had an impact on you

part3.title: Art and Society
questions: Do you think governments have a responsibility to fund the arts?
How has digital technology changed the way people create and consume art?
Do you think traditional art forms like classical music and painting are becoming less relevant in modern society?

part1.title: News and Media
questions: How do you usually get your news — through television, newspapers or online?
Do you think the news media generally reports things accurately?
How much time do you spend reading or watching the news each day?
Do you follow any particular journalists or news sources that you trust?

part2.title: Describe a news story that had a strong effect on you
part2.cue_card:
Describe a news story that had a strong effect on you.

You should say:
- What the story was about
- When and where you heard or read about it
- What happened as a result of this story
- And explain why this story had such a strong effect on you

part3.title: Media and Society
questions: Do you think social media has made it harder for people to distinguish between reliable and unreliable news?
Should governments have the power to regulate what is published on social media platforms?
How do you think the rise of artificial intelligence will affect journalism and the media industry?

part1.title: Money and Spending
questions: Are you good at managing your money?
Do you prefer to save money or spend it on experiences and things you enjoy?
Do you think young people today are taught enough about managing finances?
Has your attitude towards money changed as you have got older?

part2.title: Describe a time when you made a significant financial decision
part2.cue_card:
Describe a time when you made a significant financial decision.

You should say:
- What the decision was
- When you had to make it
- How you went about making the decision
- And explain whether you think it was the right decision in hindsight

part3.title: Economics and Inequality
questions: Do you think the gap between rich and poor is growing in most countries? What are the consequences of this?
Should wealthy individuals and corporations pay higher rates of tax to fund public services?
How do you think automation and artificial intelligence will affect economic inequality in the future?

part1.title: Food and Eating
questions: Do you enjoy cooking? What kinds of things do you like to cook?
How important is food to you in terms of your cultural identity?
Have your eating habits changed much over the years?
Do you prefer eating at home or going out to restaurants?

part2.title: Describe a meal that you particularly enjoyed
part2.cue_card:
Describe a meal that you particularly enjoyed.

You should say:
- What the meal was
- Where you had it and who you were with
- What was special about the food or the occasion
- And explain why this meal was particularly enjoyable for you

part3.title: Food, Culture and Globalisation
questions: How has globalisation affected the food people eat in your country?
Do you think the growing popularity of fast food is a problem for public health?
How important do you think it is to preserve traditional food cultures?

part1.title: Sport and Physical Activity
questions: Do you play any sports or do any physical activities regularly?
Did you play a lot of sport when you were at school?
Do you prefer watching sport or playing it yourself?
Is there a sport you have always wanted to try but never have?

part2.title: Describe a sporting event you have watched or attended
part2.cue_card:
Describe a sporting event you have watched or attended.

You should say:
- What the event was
- When and where it took place
- What happened during the event
- And explain why this sporting event was memorable for you

part3.title: Sport, Competition and Society
questions: Do you think competitive sport teaches children valuable life skills or puts too much pressure on them?
Why do you think some countries invest so heavily in elite sport and Olympic performance?
Do you think professional athletes are paid too much compared to other professions in society?

part1.title: Science and Discovery
questions: Are you interested in science? Which areas of science interest you most?
Do you follow news about scientific discoveries and breakthroughs?
Did you enjoy science subjects at school?
Do you think scientific thinking is important in everyday life?

part2.title: Describe a scientific discovery or invention that you find fascinating
part2.cue_card:
Describe a scientific discovery or invention that you find fascinating.

You should say:
- What it is
- When it was discovered or invented
- How it works or what it does
- And explain why you find it so fascinating

part3.title: Science, Ethics and Progress
questions: Do you think there are areas of scientific research that should be limited or banned for ethical reasons?
How should governments decide which areas of scientific research to prioritise and fund?
Do you think the benefits of scientific progress always outweigh the risks?

part1.title: Identity and Belonging
questions: Do you feel a strong connection to your national identity or cultural background?
Has living in your country shaped your values and outlook on life?
Do you think younger generations have a different sense of identity from older generations?
Do you feel more comfortable in large groups of people or in smaller more intimate settings?

part2.title: Describe a tradition or custom from your culture that is important to you
part2.cue_card:
Describe a tradition or custom from your culture that is important to you.

You should say:
- What the tradition or custom is
- When and how it is observed
- Who participates in it
- And explain why this tradition or custom is important to you personally

part3.title: Identity, Culture and Change
questions: Do you think globalisation is causing cultures around the world to become more similar?
How important is it for immigrant communities to maintain their cultural identity in a new country?
Do you think national identity will become more or less important in the future as globalisation continues?
